cases.title: Web service fails to bind to its configured port
cases.description: A newly deployed service refuses to start, logging "address already in use". Identify the conflicting process and resolve it.

cases.title: Explorer.exe repeatedly crashing
cases.description: A user reports Explorer restarting every few minutes after a Windows Update. Identify the offending process/module and restore stability.

cases.title: Intermittent packet loss to a remote site
cases.description: Users report intermittent slowness reaching a branch office. Trace the path and isolate where loss is occurring.

cases.title: Port 443 unreachable from one VLAN only
cases.description: A new internal HTTPS service is reachable from most VLANs but times out from one specific VLAN.

cases.title: Application log file grows without bound
cases.description: A service log has filled the disk overnight. Identify the cause, rotate the log, and prevent recurrence.

cases.title: Runaway process consuming all CPU cores
cases.description: A background job has pegged every CPU core for over an hour. Identify it, determine if it is safe to kill, and remediate.

cases.title: Backup job leaves stale lock file
cases.description: A nightly backup job fails with "lock file exists" after a previous run was killed mid-job.

cases.title: C: drive nearly full on a workstation
cases.description: A workstation is showing low-disk-space warnings. Identify large/unnecessary files and free space safely.

cases.title: Time drift causing authentication failures
cases.description: Several servers are intermittently failing Kerberos/SSO authentication. Suspect clock drift between hosts.

cases.title: Suspected routing loop between two sites
cases.description: Two sites occasionally lose all connectivity with a spike in latency just before. A routing loop is suspected.

cases.title: "Permission denied" writing to shared directory
cases.description: A user cannot write to a shared project directory despite being in the right group. Investigate ownership, group, and ACLs.

cases.title: Web service fails to bind to its configured port
cases.description: A newly deployed service refuses to start, logging "address already in use". Identify the conflicting process and resolve it.

cases.title: Runaway process consuming all CPU cores
cases.description: A background job has pegged every CPU core for over an hour. Identify it, determine if it is safe to kill, and remediate.

cases.title: Windows host cannot reach the file server
cases.description: A workstation can browse the internet but cannot reach an internal file server by hostname. Diagnose connectivity and name resolution.

cases.title: Time drift causing authentication failures
cases.description: Several servers are intermittently failing Kerberos/SSO authentication. Suspect clock drift between hosts.

cases.title: Suspected routing loop between two sites
cases.description: Two sites occasionally lose all connectivity with a spike in latency just before. A routing loop is suspected.

cases.title: Application crashes due to full disk
cases.description: An application crashes on write with "No space left on device". Find what is consuming disk space and free it safely.

cases.title: New employee account missing required group
cases.description: A new hire cannot access a shared resource because their account is missing from the expected group.

cases.title: Service fails to start after package upgrade
cases.description: After a routine package upgrade, a critical service no longer starts. Check unit status, logs, and config compatibility.

cases.title: Explorer.exe repeatedly crashing
cases.description: A user reports Explorer restarting every few minutes after a Windows Update. Identify the offending process/module and restore stability.

cases.title: User locked out of domain account
cases.description: A user is locked out after multiple failed login attempts from an unfamiliar device. Investigate and unlock safely.

cases.description: After a routine package upgrade, a critical service no longer starts. Check unit status, logs, and config compatibility.
